title: apium nodiflorum var. ochreatum
intro: Apium nodiflorum var. ochreatum, also known as Ochre Sea Celery, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the carrot family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
